definition: Term premium has a structural fiscal supply floor that persists independently of monetary policy. Driven by $2-2.5T annual gross issuance, with the curve belly (5-7Y) most vulnerable as it has neither Fed anchoring nor structural pension demand. TBAC composition decisions are dominant event risk. Current levels may be historically normal but the drivers (fiscal supply saturation) are structurally different from pre-GFC.
evidence: 5-7Y belly absorbs supply with neither Fed anchoring nor structural pension demand
TBAC composition decisions as dominant event risk
Pair_3 produced productive tension on normalization vs elevation framing
Both level (historically normal) and drivers (structurally different) framings partially correct
open_questions: Is the current term premium floor a return to pre-GFC normal or a new regime driven by fiscal saturation?
How does the T-bill share normalization from 22% toward coupon bonds affect the long-end term premium?
